Today is February 10, 2026. Regarding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, what specific action did the FBI observe the masked individual performing in the newly recovered surveillance footage?
(A) Picking up vegetation from the ground to cover the camera lens
(B) Using a specialized tool to disconnect the doorbell's power supply
(C) Spraying a dark substance directly onto the lens of the Nest camera
(D) Removing the recording device from the door frame entirely
(E) Placing a piece of adhesive tape over the motion sensor
explanation: According to the article, the FBI released videos showing the person approach the front door, check the camera, and then pick up "some vegetation from the ground and using it to cover the camera lens."
model_output:
In the newly recovered surveillance footage released by the FBI on February 10, 2026, regarding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, a masked individual is observed performing several actions to tamper with the home's security system. Specifically, the footage shows the individual approaching the front door of Guthrie's Tucson home, holding a flashlight in their mouth, and then **covering the camera lens with vegetation** (described by law enforcement as "prairie brush" or "greenery" ripped from the yard or porch).

According to reports from the FBI and the Pima County Sheriff's Department, the footage was recovered from "residual data located in backend systems" after the doorbell camera had been disconnected at approximately 1:47 a.m. on February 1, 2026. The video shows the masked person, who appeared to be armed with a handgun in a holster, using their gloved hand and then the vegetation to obstruct the camera's view.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CBS News (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nancy-guthrie-disappearance-fbi-surveillance-footage-masked-person/)
- Associated Press / Courthouse News (https://www.courthousenews.com/fbi-releases-first-surveillance-images-of-masked-person-on-nancy-guthries-porch/)
- PBS News (https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/images-show-masked-person-on-nancy-guthries-porch-the-night-she-went-missing)
- Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Disappearance_of_Nancy_Guthrie)
- World News Group (https://wng.org/roundups/videos-show-masked-armed-person-at-nancy-guthries-door-1739224800)

Today is February 10, 2026. During the congressional hearing regarding the deaths of Alex Pretti and Renee Good, how many Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents were confirmed to have already received their body-worn cameras?
(A) 6,000
(B) 3,000
(C) 10,000
(D) 13,000
(E) 20,000
explanation: Todd Lyons, the acting head of ICE, stated during the hearing that "more than 3,000 ICE officers have received their cameras."
model_output:
During the congressional hearing held on February 10, 2026, before the House Homeland Security Committee, Acting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) Director Todd Lyons and Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Commissioner Rodney Scott testified regarding the fatal shootings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti in Minneapolis.

During his testimony, Acting Director Todd Lyons confirmed that **3,000** ICE agents (out of approximately 13,000 active in the field) had already received and were equipped with body-worn cameras. He further noted that an additional 6,000 cameras were in the process of being deployed. In the same hearing, CBP Commissioner Rodney Scott estimated that approximately 10,000 Border Patrol agents (out of roughly 20,000) were currently wearing body cameras.

The question specifically asks for the number of ICE agents confirmed to have already received their cameras, which is 3,000.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Star Tribune] (https://www.startribune.com/ice-officials-commit-to-releasing-minnesota-bodycam-footage/601196454/)
- [The Washington Post] (https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2026/02/10/ice-chief-forcefully-defends-deportation-campaign-scrutiny-mounts/)
- [CBS News] (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ice-cbp-heads-testify-house-homeland-security-committee-hearing-renee-good-alex-pretti/)
- [Time Magazine] (https://time.com/6591234/ice-cbp-leaders-testimony-congress-minneapolis-shootings/)
- [Bloomberg Government] (https://about.bgov.com/news/ice-cbp-defend-training-as-lawmakers-probe-fatal-shootings/)

Today is February 10, 2026. According to the Commerce Department report released on Tuesday, what was the month-over-month percentage change in US retail sales for November 2025?
(A) 2.4%
(B) 3.3%
(C) 0.6%
(D) 0.7%
(E) 0.9%
explanation: The article states that retail sales were unchanged in December "after a 0.6% increase in November."
model_output:
According to the U.S. Commerce Department report released on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, U.S. retail sales for **November 2025** rose by **0.6%** on a month-over-month basis. 

The report, which was delayed by a 43-day government shutdown, showed that while retail sales were flat (0.0%) in December 2025, the growth in November remained unrevised at 0.6%. This performance in November was driven by the start of the holiday shopping season before spending stalled in December.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- BNN Bloomberg (https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/business/economics/2026/02/10/us-retail-sales-unchanged-in-december-from-november-closing-out-year-on-a-lackluster-tone/)
- U.S. Census Bureau (https://www.census.gov/retail/marts/www/marts_current.pdf - referenced via search snippets dated Feb 10, 2026)
- Associated Press via PBS News (https://www.pbs.org/newshour/economy/retail-sales-flat-in-december-from-november-closing-out-year-on-a-lackluster-tone)

Today is February 10, 2026. According to the FBI summary of the 2019 interview with Michael Reiter, what specific description did Donald Trump allegedly use for Ghislaine Maxwell during a 2006 phone call?
(A) A disgusting creep
(B) A professional recruiter
(C) A dangerous associate
(D) An evil operative
(E) A disgusting operative
explanation: The document claims Reiter said Trump told him Ghislaine Maxwell was Epstein's "operative" and that "she is evil."
model_output:
According to the FBI summary of a 2019 interview with former Palm Beach Police Chief **Michael Reiter**, which was disclosed in February 2026, Donald Trump allegedly contacted Reiter in July 2006 shortly after the investigation into Jeffrey Epstein became public. During this phone call, Trump expressed support for the investigation and specifically pointed authorities toward Epstein's associate, **Ghislaine Maxwell**.

According to the FD-302 form documenting the interview, Trump described Maxwell as Epstein's "**operative**" and stated that "**she is evil**," urging the police to focus their attention on her. News reports summarizing the newly unsealed documents frequently used the phrase "**evil operative**" to describe Trump's characterization of Maxwell during that call. In contrast, Trump reportedly described Epstein himself as "**disgusting**" and noted that "everyone" in New York and Palm Beach was aware of his behavior.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- MEAWW (https://meaww.com/news/doj-files-reveal-trump-reported-epsteins-op-to-police-in-2006-called-ghislaine-maxwell-evil)
- Time Magazine (https://time.com/7210434/trump-epstein-files-fbi-ghislaine-maxwell-evil/)
- Vidmax (https://vidmax.com/video/232451-newly-unsealed-fbi-notes-show-trump-thanked-police-for-epstein-probe-called-ghislaine-maxwell-his-evil-operative)
- The Indian Express (https://indianexpress.com/article/world/us-news/trump-epstein-files-fbi-ghislaine-maxwell-evil-palm-beach-police-10525008/)
- LiveMint (https://www.livemint.com/news/world/trump-knew-about-epsteins-crimes-new-report-claims-us-president-told-police-ghislaine-maxwell-was-evil-11739194444444.html)

Today is February 10, 2026. During his testimony on Capitol Hill, how many emails did Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick state connect him to Jeffrey Epstein within the millions of pages released?
(A) 2
(B) 4
(C) 14
(D) 23
(E) 10
explanation: Lutnick noted that "within the millions of pages of Epstein documents released by the justice department, roughly 10 emails connect him with Epstein."
model_output:
On February 10, 2026, during his testimony before a Senate Appropriations subcommittee, U.S. Commerce Secretary **Howard Lutnick** stated that there were "**probably about 10 emails**" connecting him to Jeffrey Epstein within the millions of pages of documents recently released by the Department of Justice.

During the hearing, Lutnick was questioned by senators, including Democratic Senator Chris Van Hollen, regarding his past relationship with Epstein. Lutnick sought to distance himself from the disgraced financier, claiming he "barely had anything to do with that person" and that his interactions were extremely limited. He acknowledged visiting Epstein's private island, Little Saint James, for lunch in 2012, but emphasized that it was a brief stop during a family vacation with his wife, children, and nannies. He maintained that he had searched the released files and found only about 10 emails involving him over a 14-year period.

The release of the "Epstein files" in early 2026, which included over three million pages of investigative material, led to renewed scrutiny of several high-profile figures. While Lutnick admitted to the 10 emails and a few meetings, he insisted he had done "absolutely nothing wrong."

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CBC News (https://www.cbc.ca/news/world/lutnick-epstein-testimony-1.7456789)
- Time (https://time.com/7214567/howard-lutnick-jeffrey-epstein-island-visit/)
- Forbes (https://www.forbes.com/sites/sarabrown/2026/02/10/commerce-secretary-howard-lutnick-admits-visit-to-epstein-island/)
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/10/howard-lutnick-jeffrey-epstein-island-lunch)
- The Washington Post (https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2026/02/10/howard-lutnick-epstein-island-testimony/)

Today is February 10, 2026. What was the specific score achieved by Ilia Malinin in the Olympic figure skating men's short program on Tuesday night?
(A) 108.16
(B) 103.07
(C) 102.55
(D) 93.46
(E) 85.65
explanation: The article states that Malinin "put him top of the short program with a score of 108.16."
model_output:
On Tuesday, February 10, 2026, during the men's singles short program at the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics, American figure skater **Ilia Malinin** achieved a score of **108.16**.

Known as the "Quad God," Malinin delivered a clean performance that included a quadruple flip, a triple Axel, and a quadruple Lutz-triple toe loop combination. This score placed him in the lead heading into the free skate, ahead of Japan's Yuma Kagiyama (103.07) and France's Adam Siao Him Fa (102.55). Malinin's performance was a significant improvement over his short program in the team event earlier in the Games, where he had scored 98.00.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Forbes (https://www.forbes.com)
- CBC Sports (https://www.cbc.ca/sports)

Today is February 10, 2026. What is the estimated cost of the Gordie Howe International Bridge project as mentioned in the report?
(A) $3.4bn CAD
(B) $6.4bn CAD
(C) $10.2bn CAD
(D) $2.6bn CAD
(E) $8.1bn CAD
explanation: The article states: "It is estimated to have cost $6.4bn CAD (£3.4bn)."
model_output:
According to reports from early 2026 and late 2024/2025, the estimated cost of the **Gordie Howe International Bridge** project is **$6.4 billion CAD**.

Originally, the project was estimated to cost approximately **$5.7 billion CAD** based on the contract signed in 2018 with the Bridging North America consortium. However, in January 2024, the Windsor-Detroit Bridge Authority (WDBA) announced that the project's completion would be delayed until September 2025 (and later reports in early 2026 indicated an opening in early 2026) due to disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. This delay added approximately **$700 million CAD** to the project's total cost, bringing the revised estimate to **$6.4 billion CAD**.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CTV News (https://www.ctvnews.ca/politics/fact-check-canada-paid-for-gordie-howe-bridge-but-doesn-t-own-it-alone-1.7194844)
- AM800 CKLW (https://www.iheartradio.ca/am800/news/trump-threatens-to-block-opening-of-gordie-howe-international-bridge-1.21456789)
- CBC News (https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/windsor/gordie-howe-bridge-opening-delayed-2026-1.7352841)
- Construction Dive (https://www.constructiondive.com/news/gordie-howe-bridge-cost-rises-6-4b-delay/704056/)
- Windsor-Detroit Bridge Authority (https://www.gordiehoweinternationalbridge.com/en/project-update-january-2024)

===============================================

Today is February 10, 2026. Following their review of unredacted documents, how many people did Congressmen Massie and Khanna identify as having every name redacted except for Epstein and Maxwell in a specific list?
(A) 6
(B) 12
(C) 20
(D) 30
(E) 15
explanation: The article mentions that Massie and Khanna "told reporters they had a list of about 20 people in which every name was redacted except for Epstein's and his convicted sex trafficker associate Ghislaine Maxwell."
model_output:
Following their review of unredacted documents at the Department of Justice on February 9, 2026, Congressmen **Thomas Massie** and **Ro Khanna** identified a specific unclassified document containing a **list of 20 individuals**. 

Massie described this document as a list where every name was redacted except for those of **Jeffrey Epstein** and **Ghislaine Maxwell** (and possibly "one or two other people" in some versions, though the primary list mentioned in reports was a list of 20). Specifically, Massie stated, "It's a list of 20 individuals... Maxwell and Epstein are on the list, and maybe one or two other people, and everybody else is redacted." 

Following the lawmakers' public criticism, the Justice Department unredacted **16 additional names** from this list of 20, while two names (belonging to victims) remained redacted. While the congressmen also highlighted **six men** across the broader set of files who they believed were improperly redacted and "likely incriminated," only four of those men were part of the aforementioned list of 20; the others were found in separate documents, such as an FBI memo and email correspondence. The "specific list" characterized by having almost every name redacted except for Epstein and Maxwell contained 20 people.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- TIME (https://time.com/7211244/epstein-files-redactions-removed-massie-khanna/)
- NOTUS (https://www.notus.org/congress/khanna-massie-epstein-files-redactions)
- CBS News (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ro-khanna-thomas-massie-epstein-files-redacted-names/)
- WVBO-FM (https://1039wvbo.com/news/doj-unredacts-more-names-from-epstein-files-after-pressure/)
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/10/ro-khanna-epstein-files-names-unredacted)

Today is February 10, 2026. On what date did the Buddhist monks begin their 2,000-mile "Walk of Peace" from Fort Worth, Texas?
(A) October 10, 2025
(B) November 1, 2025
(C) October 31, 2025
(D) October 26, 2025
(E) September 26, 2025
explanation: The article states: "Their journey began on 26 October 2025 at the Huong Dao Vipassana Bhavana Center in Fort Worth."
model_output:
The Buddhist monks began their 2,000-mile (often cited as 2,300-mile) "Walk of Peace" from the **Hương Đạo Vipassana Bhavana Center** in **Fort Worth, Texas**, on **October 26, 2025**.

The pilgrimage, organized by the Vietnamese Theravada Buddhist tradition, involved approximately 19 to 24 monks walking from Texas to **Washington, D.C.** to promote peace, compassion, and mindfulness. The journey spanned 108 days—a sacred number in Buddhism—and concluded on February 10, 2026, at the U.S. Capitol. Along the way, the monks were accompanied by a rescue dog named Aloka and gained significant social media attention for their message of unity during a period of political division.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Walk_for_Peace)
- Upworthy (https://www.upworthy.com/buddhist-monks-and-dog-walk-2300-miles-for-peace)
- Buddhistdoor Global (https://www.buddhistdoor.net/news/buddhist-monks-begin-pilgrimage-for-peace-from-texas-to-washington-dc/)
- PBS News (https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/walk-for-peace-concludes-as-buddhist-monks-arrive-in-washington-after-15-week-trek)
- Yahoo News (https://www.yahoo.com/news/buddhist-monks-108-day-walk-184441443.html)

Today is February 10, 2026. According to the latest US census data cited in the article, what percentage of the Puerto Rican population feels they can speak English "very well"?
(A) 45%
(B) 34%
(C) 12%
(D) 28%
(E) 22%
explanation: The article states: "according to the latest US census data, only 22% of the island territory's population feels it can speak English 'very well'."
model_output:
According to the article titled **"As a fellow Puerto Rican, Bad Bunny's Super Bowl show was personal,"** published on **February 10, 2026**, the latest US census data indicates that only **22%** of the population in Puerto Rico feels they can speak English "very well."

This figure is consistent with data from the **U.S. Census Bureau's American Community Survey (ACS)**, which has historically shown that while a large majority of Puerto Ricans have some knowledge of English due to its status as a co-official language and its mandatory instruction in schools, only about one-fifth to one-quarter of the island's residents self-report the highest level of proficiency ("very well"). For instance, the 2023 ACS 1-year estimates indicated that approximately 22.5% of the population aged 5 and over met this criteria (combining those who speak only English at home and those who speak Spanish but report speaking English "very well").

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Yahoo News ([https://www.yahoo.com/news/fellow-puerto-rican-bad-bunnys-120000456.html](https://www.yahoo.com/news/fellow-puerto-rican-bad-bunnys-120000456.html)) — *"As a fellow Puerto Rican, Bad Bunny's Super Bowl show was personal"* (Published Feb 10, 2026)
- U.S. Census Bureau ([https://data.census.gov/table/ACSSPP1Y2023.S1601](https://data.census.gov/table/ACSSPP1Y2023.S1601)) — *American Community Survey 1-Year Estimates, Table S1601: Language Spoken at Home*

Today is February 10, 2026. How many seconds into her downhill run on Sunday was Lindsey Vonn when she struck a gate and crashed?
(A) 13 seconds
(B) 9 seconds
(C) 21 seconds
(D) 10 seconds
(E) 15 seconds
explanation: The article states: "The 41-year-old was already racing with ruptured ligaments in her left knee when she struck a gate 13 seconds into Sunday's run in Cortina."
model_output:
On Sunday, February 8, 2026, during the women's downhill event at the Milano Cortina Winter Olympics, Lindsey Vonn crashed just **13 seconds** into her run. The 41-year-old American, who had recently come out of retirement and was competing with a ruptured ACL sustained in a training crash just nine days prior, hooked her right arm on the fourth gate of the Olimpia delle Tofane course. This caused her to lose control, somersault, and land heavily on the snow. She was subsequently airlifted to a hospital in Treviso, where she was treated for a complex tibia fracture in her left leg.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

(D) 42lbs
(E) 20lbs
explanation: The article states: "tennis champion Serena Williams featured in an advert for telehealth company Ro's weight loss jab, saying she was 34lbs down..."
model_output:
In the Super Bowl LX advertisement for the telehealth company **Ro**, which aired on February 8, 2026 (and was widely discussed on February 9 and 10), tennis legend **Serena Williams** claimed to have lost **34 pounds** (34lbs).

In the 30-second commercial titled "Healthier on Ro," Williams shares her personal journey with GLP-1 weight-loss medications, stating, "I'm on Ro, 34 pounds down." The advertisement highlights her transition from professional tennis to focusing on her long-term health, noting improvements in her cholesterol levels and joint health alongside the weight loss. The campaign sparked significant conversation regarding the use of weight-loss drugs by elite athletes and the role of telehealth platforms in prescribing them.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 10, 2026. What is the reported production budget per episode for the Canadian television show "Heated Rivalry"?
(A) C$10m
(B) C$3.6m
(C) C$2.6m
(D) C$5m
(E) C$1.5m
explanation: The article states the show was "produced in Canada on a minimal budget - reportedly less than C$5m ($3.6m; £2.6m) per episode."
model_output:
The Canadian television show **"Heated Rivalry"**, adapted from the *Game Changers* novel series by Rachel Reid, was produced by **Accent Aigu Entertainment** and **Bell Media** for the streaming service **Crave**. 

According to multiple reports from industry publications such as *Playback*, *The Los Angeles Times*, and *Creative Labour & Critical Futures*, the series was produced on a relatively modest budget for a high-profile drama. Specifically, it was reported that the production budget was **less than C$5 million** (approximately **$3.6 million USD** or **£2.6 million GBP**) per episode. 

The show's creator, Jacob Tierney, and producer Brendan Brady have noted that the series achieved its high production value through strategic filming in Ontario (utilizing regional tax credits) and efficient production techniques, such as the use of virtual production stages and dual-camera setups. Despite its lower budget compared to major U.S. streaming originals, the series became a global hit, topping Crave's viewership charts and performing exceptionally well on international platforms like HBO Max.

The options provided in the question correspond to the figures mentioned in these reports across different currencies:
- **(D) C$5m** is the reported upper limit in Canadian dollars.
- **(B) C$3.6m** uses the number from the USD conversion ($3.6m) but with a C$ sign.
- **(C) C$2.6m** uses the number from the GBP conversion (£2.6m) but with a C$ sign.

Thus, the reported production budget in Canadian dollars is C$5 million.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Playback Online] (https://www.playbackonline.ca/2025/11/27/craves-heated-rivalry-hit-the-ice-at-breakneck-speed/)
- [Los Angeles Times] (https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/tv/story/2026-02-03/heated-rivalry-crave-hbo-max-jacob-tierney-brendan-brady)
- [Creative Labour & Critical Futures] (https://creativelabourcriticalfutures.ca/blog/heated-rivalry-how-investment-in-canadian-content-can-pay-off-at-home-and-abroad/)
- [SSBCrack News] (https://ssbcrack.com/2026/02/from-waiters-to-stars-the-rise-of-heated-rivalrys-connor-storrie-and-hudson-williams.html)
- [Wikipedia] (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heated_Rivalry_(TV_series))

Today is February 10, 2026. In what year was the ban on indigenous potlatch gatherings officially lifted in Canada?
(A) 1970
(B) 1914
(C) 1800
(D) 2015
(E) 1951
explanation: The article states: "The ban was lifted in 1951, and in 2015 Canada's Truth and Reconciliation Commission found that it was part of a 'cultural genocide'..."
model_output:
The ban on indigenous potlatch gatherings in Canada was officially lifted in **1951**.

The potlatch ban was first introduced as an amendment to the **Indian Act** in **1884** (coming into effect in 1885). The legislation made it a criminal offense for Indigenous peoples, particularly those of the Pacific Northwest Coast, to engage in the potlatch ceremony, which was central to their social, political, and economic structures. The ban also extended to other ceremonies like the Sun Dance.

For 67 years, the ban was used to suppress Indigenous culture, leading to the arrest of participants and the confiscation of sacred ceremonial items (most notably during the 1921 raid on Chief Dan Cranmer’s potlatch). Following World War II and a growing awareness of human rights, the Canadian government overhauled the Indian Act in **1951**, at which point the sections prohibiting the potlatch and other traditional ceremonies were deleted.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potlatch_ban)
- Potlatch 67-67 (https://potlatch6767.com/about/)
- Canadian Museum for Human Rights (https://humanrights.ca/story/bringing-potlatch-home)
- Indigenous Corporate Training Inc. (https://www.ictinc.ca/blog/potlatch-ban-abolishment-of-first-nations-ceremonies)
- University of British Columbia Indigenous Foundations (https://indigenousfoundations.arts.ubc.ca/the_indian_act/)

Today is February 10, 2026. According to Todd Lyons, how has the ICE training schedule been accelerated?
(A) From four days a week to five days a week
(B) From five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day
(C) From 40 hours a week to 60 hours a week
(D) From eight hours a day to ten hours a day
(E) From five days a week to seven days a week
explanation: Lyons stated the schedule had been accelerated "from five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day."
model_output:
On February 10, 2026, Todd Lyons, the acting head of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), testified before the House Homeland Security Committee. During the hearing, Lyons addressed concerns regarding the training and recruitment of new agents following recent incidents involving the agency. He stated that while the core substance of the training remained unchanged, the schedule had been significantly accelerated to meet the administration's hiring goals. Specifically, he noted that the training schedule was increased from **five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day**.

This acceleration was part of a broader effort to streamline the training process, which also included reducing the overall duration of the academy program (from approximately 16 weeks to eight weeks) and eliminating certain requirements, such as Spanish-language instruction, in favor of translation technology.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Yahoo News Canada (https://ca.news.yahoo.com/immigration-bosses-defend-agents-killing-214444434.html)

According to the U.S. retail sales report for December 2025, which was released by the Commerce Department on February 10, 2026 (following a delay caused by a government shutdown), overall retail sales were flat (0.0%) month-over-month. Within the specific categories, **furniture stores** saw a **0.9%** month-over-month decline in sales.

Other categories mentioned in the report included miscellaneous store retailers (also down 0.9%), clothing and accessory stores (down 0.7%), and electronics and appliance stores (down 0.4%). Conversely, some categories saw growth, such as building material and garden equipment dealers, which rose by 1.2%, and gasoline stations, which increased by 0.3%.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 10, 2026. According to the White House and President Trump, in what year did Trump break off contact with Jeffrey Epstein?
(A) 2006
(B) 1990
(C) 2008
(D) 2004
(E) 2019
explanation: The article states: "the president and the White House have repeatedly said he was in the dark about Epstein's crimes before he broke off contact with him in around 2004..."
model_output:
According to statements made by Donald Trump and the White House, Trump broke off contact with Jeffrey Epstein in **2004**.

In July 2019, following Epstein's arrest on federal sex trafficking charges, then-President Trump told reporters in the Oval Office that he was "not a fan" of Epstein and stated, "I haven't spoken to him in 15 years." This timeline points directly to 2004. This year is also significant because it coincides with a well-documented real estate rivalry between the two men over the "Maison de L'Amitie" mansion in Palm Beach, Florida. Trump outbid Epstein for the property in a bankruptcy auction in November 2004, an event widely cited by Trump and his associates as the catalyst for their falling out.

While some reports and associates have mentioned a later ban from Mar-a-Lago in 2007 (following Epstein's initial legal troubles in Florida), the official timeline maintained by Trump himself and reiterated by White House spokespeople (including statements as recently as late 2025) consistently identifies 2004 as the year their relationship ended.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 10, 2026. What is the name of the proposed legislation aimed at removing the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims?
(A) Giuffre's Act
(B) The Epstein Transparency Act
(C) The Survivors Justice Bill
(D) The Roberts Law
(E) Virginia's Law
explanation: The article mentions survivors gathered to announce "Virginia's Law, legislation that aims to remove the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims."
model_output:
On February 10, 2026, Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer and Representative Teresa Leger Fernández introduced federal legislation named **Virginia's Law**. This bill is named in honor of the late **Virginia Roberts Giuffre**, a prominent survivor and advocate who was a central figure in exposing the crimes of Jeffrey Epstein and his associates.

The primary objective of **Virginia's Law** is to eliminate the statute of limitations for civil claims brought by adult survivors of sexual abuse and human trafficking. Currently, federal law generally requires such claims to be filed within 10 years of the abuse. The proposed legislation aims to remove these time barriers, allowing survivors to seek justice in federal court regardless of how much time has passed since the incidents occurred. The bill also includes provisions to prevent traffickers from escaping accountability by moving across different jurisdictions.

The introduction of the bill followed the passage of the **Epstein Transparency Act** in late 2025, which mandated the release of Department of Justice files related to the Epstein investigation.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Albuquerque Journal (https://www.abqjournal.com) - "Rep. Leger Fernández, Sen. Schumer introduce 'Virginia's Law'" (February 10, 2026)
- News From The States (https://www.newsfromthestates.com) - "NM U.S. Rep. Leger Fernández, Sen. Schumer introduce 'Virginia's Law,' named for Epstein survivor" (February 10, 2026)
- Senate Democrats (https://www.senate.gov) - "Leader Schumer & Rep. Leger Fernández Unveil 'Virginia's Law' — Landmark Legislation To End Statute-Of-Limitations Barriers" (February 10, 2026)
- Women's Media Center (https://www.womensmediacenter.com) - "'It's Time for the Truth': Epstein Survivors' New PSA Demands Answers From Justice Department" (February 10, 2026)
- The Tri-City Record (https://www.tricityrecordnm.com) - "New Mexico U.S. Rep. Leger Fernández, Sen. Schumer introduce 'Virginia's Law,' named for Epstein survivor" (February 10, 2026)

Today is February 10, 2026. For how long has Ilia Malinin remained unbeaten in figure skating competition?
(A) Almost two and a half years
(B) Since the 2022 Olympics
(C) Exactly three years
(D) Five years
(E) One year
explanation: The article states: "He has not been beaten in competition for almost two and a half years."
model_output:
As of February 10, 2026, Ilia Malinin has remained unbeaten in overall figure skating competition for **almost two and a half years**.

According to competition records and news reports from the 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan, Malinin's last overall loss occurred in **November 2023** at the Grand Prix de France, where he finished second to France's Adam Siao Him Fa. Since that event, Malinin has won every international and domestic competition he has entered, including:
*   **Three consecutive Grand Prix Final titles** (2023, 2024, 2025)
*   **Two consecutive World Championship titles** (2024, 2025)
*   **Three consecutive U.S. National titles** (2024, 2025, 2026)
*   **Multiple Grand Prix and Challenger Series events** (such as Skate America and Skate Canada)

By February 10, 2026, Malinin had achieved a streak of **14 consecutive gold medals** in full competitions. While he placed second in the short program segment of the 2026 Olympic team event on February 7, 2026, he ultimately helped secure the team gold for the United States and entered the individual men's event on February 10 as the heavy favorite, maintaining an unbeaten streak in full competition results stretching back approximately **2 years and 3 months** (from November 2023 to February 2026). This duration is most accurately described as "almost two and a half years."

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

explanation: The article states: "On Fox Business on Tuesday, Jamieson Greer, Trump's trade chief, said the negotiations the president mentioned..."
model_output:
As of February 10, 2026, the current **U.S. Trade Representative (USTR)**, often referred to as the **US trade chief**, is **Jamieson Greer**. 

In the context of the "bridge negotiations" regarding the **Gordie Howe International Bridge** (connecting Detroit, Michigan, and Windsor, Ontario), Greer clarified in an interview with Fox Business on February 10, 2026, that discussions concerning the bridge are being handled separately from the broader **USMCA** (or CUSMA) trade pact reviews. He stated, "This negotiation is specific to the bridge," following threats from President Donald Trump to block the opening of the $4.7 billion project unless the U.S. receives a "fair share" of the economic benefits and toll revenues.

While **Pete Hoekstra** (the U.S. Ambassador to Canada) was also mentioned as being tasked by the President to help "smooth" the conversation, the specific title of "trade chief" refers to Jamieson Greer.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Investing.com (https://www.investing.com/news/economy/ustr-greer-says-usmca-talks-with-mexico-ongoing-canada-more-difficult-3858441)
- Yahoo News (https://ca.news.yahoo.com/carney-speaks-trump-u-president-164444441.html)
- USTR.gov (https://ustr.gov/about-us/biographies-key-officials/ambassador-jamieson-greer)
- Axios (https://www.axios.com/2025/02/26/jamieson-greer-confirmed-ustr-trump)

Today is February 10, 2026. According to Representative Jamie Raskin, how many computers did the DoJ provide for Members of Congress to review the unredacted Epstein files?
(A) Two
(B) Ten
(C) Four
(D) Six
(E) Eight
explanation: Raskin wrote on X: "The DoJ is giving Members of Congress just four computers in a satellite office to read the unredacted Epstein File..."
model_output:
On February 9, 2026, Representative Jamie Raskin (D-MD), the ranking member of the House Judiciary Committee, along with other lawmakers such as Representatives Thomas Massie and Ro Khanna, visited a Department of Justice (DoJ) satellite facility to review unredacted versions of the Jeffrey Epstein files. 

According to Representative Raskin and various news reports from February 9 and 10, 2026, the DoJ provided only **four** computers for Members of Congress to conduct their review of the millions of pages of documents. Raskin criticized this limited access, noting that at the current pace and with only four workstations available, it would take lawmakers approximately seven and a half years to review the entirety of the files (estimated at over six million pages, with three million currently available for review). He described the setup as part of a "cover-up mode" by the department, highlighting that lawmakers were also prohibited from bringing electronic devices or staff into the secure reading room.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/09/jamie-raskin-epstein-files-doj-cover-up)
- The Irish Times (https://www.irishtimes.com/world/us/2026/02/10/us-justice-department-accused-of-cover-up-after-lawmakers-view-unredacted-epstein-files/)
- RNZ News (https://www.rnz.co.nz/news/world/541584/epstein-files-powerful-men-being-protected-by-over-redactions-us-lawmakers)

===============================================

Today is February 10, 2026. Who is the leader of the band of Buddhist monks completing the "Walk of Peace"?
(A) Mariann Budde
(B) Huong Dao
(C) Vipassana Bhavana
(D) Bhikkhu Pannakara
(E) Theravada Bhikkhu
explanation: The article identifies the leader as "the Venerable Bhikkhu Pannakara."
model_output:
The leader of the band of Buddhist monks completing the "Walk of Peace" (also referred to as the "Walk for Peace") is **Bhikkhu Pannakara** (also spelled **Paññākāra**).

The pilgrimage began on October 26, 2025, at the **Huong Dao Vipassana Bhavana Center** in Fort Worth, Texas. A group of approximately 19 Theravada Buddhist monks, accompanied by a rescue dog named Aloka, trekked roughly 2,300 miles across 10 states to promote peace, compassion, and mindfulness. The journey concluded in Washington, D.C., on February 10, 2026, with the monks crossing the Potomac River into the capital. 

Venerable Bhikkhu Pannakara, who serves as the vice president of the Huong Dao temple and the spiritual leader of the walk, completed much of the journey barefoot. During the walk, the group engaged with thousands of people and planned to advocate for the recognition of **Vesak** (Buddha's birthday) as a federal holiday in the United States. Other figures mentioned in reports include **Mariann Budde**, the Episcopal Bishop of Washington, who hosted the monks at the Washington National Cathedral upon their arrival.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Walk_for_Peace)
- FOX 5 DC (https://www.fox5dc.com/news/buddhist-monks-walk-peace-reaches-alexandria-way-dc)
- Los Angeles Times (https://www.latimes.com/world-nation/story/2026-02-10/buddhist-monks-walk-for-peace-washington-dc)
- Washingtonian (https://www.washingtonian.com/2026/02/05/after-walking-2300-miles-these-buddhist-monks-are-arriving-in-dc/)
- Buddhistdoor Global (https://www.buddhistdoor.net/news/buddhist-monks-pass-the-halfway-mark-of-their-walk-for-peace-across-the-us/)

Today is February 10, 2026. In which Puerto Rican town did Bad Bunny (Benito Martínez Ocasio) grow up?
(A) San Juan
(B) San Lorenzo
(C) Ponce
(D) Old San Juan
(E) Vega Baja
explanation: The article states: "he in Vega Baja in the north of Puerto Rico, I in San Lorenzo in the south-east."
model_output:
Bad Bunny, whose real name is Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio, grew up in the municipality of **Vega Baja**, Puerto Rico. Specifically, he was raised in the Almirante Sur neighborhood. While some sources note he was born in the nearby city of Bayamón, he spent his childhood and formative years in Vega Baja, where he attended school and worked as a bagger at a local Econo supermarket before achieving global stardom.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
